future_reopen_conditions: derive a multiple-point or double-zero theorem
derive an independent Planck tangency selector
supply a retained theorem converting one-sided stability into beta_lambda(M_Pl)=0

inequality_boundary.local_condition: lambda(M_Pl)=0 and lambda below M_Pl nonnegative implies beta_lambda(M_Pl)<=0, not equality

remaining_open_premise: a new multiple-point/double-zero theorem or explicit Planck tangency selector

routes.reason: Local stability below M_Pl gives beta_lambda<=0, not beta_lambda=0.
Adding tangency beta_lambda=0 is exactly the missing selector.
Would need an added theorem that the Planck boundary is a double zero.
Using observed near-criticality would be an admitted comparator, not substrate proof.

target: derive beta_lambda(M_Pl)=0 from lambda(M_Pl)=0 plus one-sided vacuum stability
verdict: One-sided vacuum stability at a lambda=0 upper boundary gives a local inequality beta_lambda(M_Pl)<=0.  It does not force the stationary equality beta_lambda(M_Pl)=0 and therefore does not select a unique y_t.
